title: Synthetic Stablecoins and Financial Stability - Liberty Street Economics
author: trevordelaney
text: One thing is wrong in your analysis though. You are implying that ethena had to unwind their basis positions on 10/10 amplifying the moves, while on 10/10 and the following 2 days, their basis position was irrelevant. They had 9bn+ of on demand stablecoin collateral available for redemption, so from a redemption perspective they were only a wrapper on usdc and usdt. On top they have a cool down period for unwinding basis positions. The liquidation cascade didn’t happen because of ethena unwinding basis positions, it happened because of a single exchange ‘dpeg’ of usde on binance, which was probably caused by malicious attack to take advantage of the oracle design on binance. Usde was being used as collat on the exchange for leverage and is where the liquidation cascade came from, not from the basis position unwinds. On all other primary liquidity venues the price of usde remained within 30bps in line with usual usdc vs usdt.
